page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.d1ne.2xlarge is a storage-dense virtual machine instance powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2682v4 processor. It features 8 dedicated physical cores and 8 vCPUs running at a base frequency of 2.5 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The instance is configured with 32.0 GB of system memory, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. A defining characteristic of this server is its massive local storage capacity, consisting of 23,622 GB of HDD storage. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 6 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates that the compute and memory performance of the ecs.d1ne.2xlarge is generally modest. Single-core and multi-core CPU performance tests, such as stress-ng and Geekbench, place the instance in the bottom 25% of tested hardware. Memory bandwidth for large blocks performs at an average level, reaching 39.05 GB/sec for read operations and 35.66 GB/sec for write operations, though small-block cached memory bandwidth remains weak. LLM inference speeds are average for small 135M models but drop to poor levels for larger 7B models.

The primary trade-off of this instance is its reliance on older CPU architecture and HDD storage, which limits its suitability for high-performance compute or low-latency database operations. However, the massive 23.6 TB local HDD capacity offers high resource density for storage-heavy workloads. This makes the instance qualitatively cost-efficient for scenarios requiring large-volume data archiving, sequential data processing, backup repositories, or distributed file systems where storage volume is prioritized over high IOPS or rapid CPU execution.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.d1ne.2xlarge is a storage-dense instance featuring 8 dedicated Intel Xeon E5-2682v4 physical cores running at 2.5 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. It is equipped with 32.0 GB of RAM and a massive 23,622 GB of local HDD storage, supported by a 6 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Performance benchmarks indicate modest compute capabilities, with single-core and multi-core CPU metrics generally falling in the bottom 25%, while large-block memory bandwidth performs at an average level. Given its hardware profile, the instance presents a clear trade-off: it sacrifices high-speed CPU and IOPS performance in favor of massive local storage capacity. This configuration is highly suited for storage-intensive workloads such as data archiving, backup storage, and sequential data processing where volume density is prioritized over raw computational speed.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.d1ne.2xlarge is a storage-dense instance featuring 8 dedicated Intel Xeon cores, 32 GB of RAM, and 23,622 GB of local HDD storage, designed for high-capacity sequential data workloads.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.d1ne.2xlarge features 8 Intel Xeon cores, 32 GB RAM, and 23.6 TB of local HDD storage for high-capacity archiving.
tagline: A storage-dense virtual server offering massive local HDD capacity and balanced memory for large-scale data archiving and sequential processing.

32.0 GB of system memory providing 4.0 GB per core
